(born May 21, 1967) is a Canadian professional [ice hockey](/wiki/Ice_hockey "Ice hockey") coach and former professional ice hockey player. He played 17 games for the [Ottawa Senators](/wiki/Ottawa_Senators "Ottawa Senators") of the [National Hockey League](/wiki/National_Hockey_League "National Hockey League") (NHL). He was also a member of the Canadian team at the 1994 Olympics. He played most of his professional playing career in [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany") and currently is an assistant coach for [Starbulls Rosenheim](/wiki/Starbulls_Rosenheim "Starbulls Rosenheim") in Germany's third-tier [Oberliga](/wiki/Oberliga_(ice_hockey) "Oberliga (ice hockey)").

[Derek Mayer neuer CO-Trainer der Ice Tigers](http://www.donaukurier.de/nachrichten/bayern/Eishockey-DEL-Nuernberg-Derek-Mayer-neuer-CO-Trainer-der-Ice-Tigers;art591,2117597)[*[permanent dead link](/wiki/Wikipedia:Link_rot "Wikipedia:Link rot")*]

**Derek Christopher Mayer** (born May 21, 1967) is a Canadian professional [ice hockey](/wiki/Ice_hockey "Ice hockey") coach and former professional ice hockey player. He played 17 games for the [Ottawa Senators](/wiki/Ottawa_Senators "Ottawa Senators") of the [National Hockey League](/wiki/National_Hockey_League "National Hockey League") (NHL). He was also a member of the Canadian team at the 1994 Olympics. He played most of his professional playing career in [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany") and currently is an assistant coach for [Starbulls Rosenheim](/wiki/Starbulls_Rosenheim "Starbulls Rosenheim") in Germany's third-tier [Oberliga](/wiki/Oberliga_(ice_hockey) "Oberliga (ice hockey)").

## Playing career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Derek_Mayer&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Playing career")]

Drafted by the [Detroit Red Wings](/wiki/Detroit_Red_Wings "Detroit Red Wings") in the 1986 NHL draft (43 overall), he graduated from the University of Denver in 1988 and then spent some time in the Canadian national team system. Mayer then signed with AHL's [Adirondack Red Wings](/wiki/Adirondack_Red_Wings "Adirondack Red Wings") for the 1989-90 season, where he spent a total of three seasons, interrupted and followed by stints with the [San Diego Gulls](/wiki/San_Diego_Gulls "San Diego Gulls") of the IHL (1990–91 and 1991–92).

Mayer made his [NHL](/wiki/National_Hockey_League "National Hockey League") debut with the [Ottawa Senators](/wiki/Ottawa_Senators "Ottawa Senators") during the 1993-94 season. He played a total of 17 games in the NHL.

He played at the 1993 World Championships and won silver at the 1994 Olympic Games in Lillehammer, Norway, with the Canadian national team.

From 1996 to 2001, Mayer played for [Eisbären Berlin](/wiki/Eisb%C3%A4ren_Berlin "Eisbären Berlin") in the German top-flight [Deutsche Eishockey Liga](/wiki/Deutsche_Eishockey_Liga "Deutsche Eishockey Liga"), followed by four years at [EC Bad Tölz](/wiki/EC_Bad_T%C3%B6lz "EC Bad Tölz") in the German second division and one year at German Oberliga side [SC Riessersee](/wiki/SC_Riessersee "SC Riessersee").

He retired in 2006.

## Coaching career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Derek_Mayer&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Coaching career")]

Mayer began his coaching career in 2006 at [Eisbären Berlin Juniors](/w/index.php?title=Eisb%C3%A4ren_Berlin_Juniors&action=edit&redlink=1 "Eisbären Berlin Juniors (page does not exist)")[[1]](#cite_note-1) and signed on 24 June 2009 a contract as Assistant Coach of [Nürnberg Ice Tigers](/wiki/N%C3%BCrnberg_Ice_Tigers "Nürnberg Ice Tigers").[[2]](#cite_note-2) In June 2015, Mayer joined the coaching staff of [EHC Red Bull München](/wiki/EHC_M%C3%BCnchen "EHC München") in Germany as an assistant.[[3]](#cite_note-3)
